content.question: To what extent is the post-2010 weakening of complementary reforms (EU funding, stakeholder consultation, decentralization reversals) a causal driver of persistent segregation, versus a symptom of deepening political constraints on reform? Would structural track elimination be feasible and durable given current institutional context?
content.reduction_path: Reconstruct education budget allocations and program-by-program funding flows 2010–present; conduct interviews with Hungarian education officials, teacher unions, and reform advocates on political feasibility of track elimination; examine reversals of prior de-segregation rules to assess whether legal/constitutional obstacles exist to re-implementation
